bins.name: Protect Ratepayers from Cost Shifts
bins.desc: The Congress members argue that current allocation practices push data center transmission upgrade costs onto existing ratepayers, raising utility bills. They ask FERC to add explicit safeguards preventing large load customers from shifting interconnection or upgrade costs to existing customers.
quotes: Rate payers in markets with data centers have been subject to higher utility bills as the costs of transmission upgrades and expansions are distributed based on current allocation practices.
Safeguards to prevent large load customers from shifting interconnection or transmission upgrade costs onto existing rate payers.

bins.name: Large Load Upgrade Cost Responsibility
bins.desc: The filers want FERC rules that place transmission upgrade costs squarely on large load customers rather than distributing them across the existing ratepayer base under current allocation practices.
quotes: Rate payers in markets with data centers have been subject to higher utility bills as the costs of transmission upgrades and expansions are distributed based on current allocation practices.
Safeguards to prevent large load customers from shifting interconnection or transmission upgrade costs onto existing rate payers.

bins.name: Emergency Curtailment Authority
bins.desc: The Congress members point to near-miss outage events caused by data center clusters suddenly disconnecting, and ask FERC to authorize utility-administered curtailment protocols for grid emergencies. They frame controlled curtailment as a reliability safeguard, not just a cost tool.
quotes: there have been "near miss" incidents in regions with rigorous data center buildout which have raised concerns about black outs when clusters of data centers have suddenly dropped off the grid.
Authorizing utility-administered curtailment protocols during grid emergencies.

bins.name: Grid Emergency Curtailment Protocols
bins.desc: The filers explicitly request that FERC authorize utility-administered curtailment of large loads during grid emergencies, citing near-miss incidents in high data center buildout regions.
quotes: Authorizing utility-administered curtailment protocols during grid emergencies.

bins.name: Cumulative and Regional Impact Studies
bins.desc: The Congress members ask FERC to assess the aggregate effects of multiple large facilities in a single region, covering transmission congestion, local generation adequacy, and resource adequacy planning. They also want multi-party consultation before approvals that could alter demand forecasts.
quotes: Clear communication between FERC, state regulators, utilities, and local communities before approving large interconnections that may alter demand forecasts or infrastructure needs.
Cumulative impacts of multiple large facilities locating within a single region, including effects on transmission congestion, local generation adequacy, and resource adequacy planning.

bins.name: Regional Concentration Effects
bins.desc: The filers ask FERC to assess how clustering of large loads within a single region affects transmission congestion and generation adequacy, framing geographic concentration as a distinct planning problem beyond individual interconnection review.
quotes: Cumulative impacts of multiple large facilities locating within a single region, including effects on transmission congestion, local generation adequacy, and resource adequacy planning.

bins.name: Data Center Reliability Incidents
bins.desc: The Congress members cite near-miss blackout events in regions with heavy data center buildout, where clusters of facilities suddenly disconnected from the grid, as evidence that the current approach creates reliability exposure.
quotes: there have been "near miss" incidents in regions with rigorous data center buildout which have raised concerns about black outs when clusters of data centers have suddenly dropped off the grid.
Cumulative impacts of multiple large facilities locating within a single region, including effects on transmission congestion, local generation adequacy, and resource adequacy planning.

bins.name: Community and State Inclusion
bins.desc: The filers call for pre-approval communication among FERC, state regulators, utilities, and local communities, meaningful opportunities for community input, and explicit inclusion of congressional stakeholders and state PUCs in the rulemaking to keep affordability and fairness central to federal energy policy.
quotes: Clear communication between FERC, state regulators, utilities, and local communities before approving large interconnections that may alter demand forecasts or infrastructure needs.

We respectfully request that FERC include congressional stakeholders and state public utility commissions in this process to guarantee that affordability, reliability, and fairness remain a top priority of federal energy policy.
